title: Add XP coin pickups and a neon-light Store page accessible from Main Menu

requirements.text: Add a new Store page reachable at a dedicated route (e.g., "/store") and make it accessible via a clearly labeled button on the Main Menu "Menu" tab that navigates to the Store page.

quotes: 1.it can access by a button in the menu page that leads to its  store page.

acceptanceCriteria: Main menu contains a visible "Store" button on the "Menu" tab.
Clicking the "Store" button navigates to the Store page without a full page reload.
There is an obvious way to return from the Store page back to the Main Menu.

requirements.text: Implement the Store catalog UI listing neon light items purchasable with XP, with the following items and XP costs: red=100, blue=100, yellow=100, purple=100, orange=100, multicolor=1000, and white=25. Show the user’s current XP balance on the Store page.

quotes: add a store page with different types of neon lights. the colors are red, blue, yellow, purple, white, orange and multicolor neon and every single one costs 100 except for the white, which costs 25 and the currency to buy these items is xp you earn in the race and the multicolored costs 1000
make a store page so they can access the store.

acceptanceCriteria: Store page shows all 7 neon light items with the exact XP prices specified by the user.
Store page displays the player’s current XP balance (numeric) in a prominent location.
Each item shows one of these states based on backend data: "Buy" (locked), "Owned" (unlocked).
If the user has insufficient XP, the UI prevents purchase and shows an English error message (e.g., "Not enough XP").

requirements.text: Add backend support for XP as a player currency and permanent unlocks for purchased neon lights, persisted per authenticated player. Expose backend methods for: fetching current XP + owned neon lights, purchasing a neon light (deduct XP and mark owned), and crediting XP earned from races/coin pickups.

quotes: the currency to buy these items is xp you earn in the race
2.permanently unlock. 3. put xp coins that cost 1, 5, 10, and even 20.

acceptanceCriteria: Neon light purchases persist across page reloads and new sessions for the same Internet Identity principal.
Purchase attempts fail with a clear error when XP is insufficient, and do not change XP or ownership state.
A successful purchase deducts the correct XP amount and immediately reflects as owned when re-fetched.
XP balance can be increased via a dedicated backend method so the frontend can credit coin pickups.

requirements.text: Add collectible XP coins to the race track gameplay such that 1 XP and 5 XP coins appear commonly on the track, while 10 XP and 20 XP coins appear in hidden places and/or become obtainable after the player performs an "amazing stunt" during a race. Collecting a coin must automatically add its XP value to the player’s total XP (used in the Store).

quotes: put xp coins that cost 1, 5, 10, and even 20.
The 1 and 5 point xp coins can appear usually on  the track but in hidden places or after doing an amazing stunt you can find the 10 and 20 point coins

acceptanceCriteria: During a race, the player can collect XP coins and see their XP increase without needing to manually refresh.
1 XP and 5 XP coins appear in normal/on-track locations with noticeably higher frequency than 10/20 coins.
10 XP and 20 XP coins are not commonly visible on the main racing line; they are placed in hidden/off-line locations and/or appear after the stunt condition is met.
Coin collection cannot be repeated for the same coin instance (i.e., a collected coin is removed/marked collected).

requirements.text: Define and implement an in-race "amazing stunt" detection heuristic (using only in-game telemetry available in the current race implementation) that, when triggered, enables access to higher-value coins (10/20) as described by the user.

quotes: after doing an amazing stunt you can find the 10 and 20 point coins

acceptanceCriteria: There is an explicit, documented stunt trigger condition implemented in the race code (e.g., airtime + landing, speed + jump, sharp drift + speed, etc.) that can be reliably achieved in gameplay.
When the stunt triggers, at least one 10 XP or 20 XP coin becomes obtainable (e.g., spawned or revealed) during that race.
The stunt trigger does not fire continuously every frame; it has sensible gating/cooldown so it feels like an achievement.

requirements.text: Create a cohesive neon-racing visual theme for the Store experience (and any new XP/coin UI elements), using a distinct palette and typography that fits neon lights. Apply it consistently across new Store components and coin-related HUD elements without changing existing read-only UI component sources.
acceptanceCriteria: Store page has a consistent neon-inspired look (colors, spacing, typography, card styling) that is clearly differentiated from default styling.
New theme is applied consistently across Store list, XP balance display, and purchase states.
No edits are made to files under "frontend/src/components/ui" or any other immutable paths.

requirements.text: Add frontend query/mutation wiring (React Query) to load XP + owned neon lights and to purchase items, ensuring UI updates immediately after successful purchase and after XP is earned from coin pickups.
acceptanceCriteria: Store page uses React Query to fetch the current XP and owned neon lights.
After purchasing an item, the Store UI updates to show the new XP total and the item as owned without requiring a hard refresh.
After collecting XP coins in a race, the player’s XP displayed in the Store is correct after navigating to the Store (and/or after a refetch).

requirements.text: Add generated static image assets for the Store neon light thumbnails and XP coin visuals, and render them in the Store page and in-race UI as appropriate. Store images must be placed under "frontend/public/assets/generated" and referenced directly by the frontend (not served through the backend).
acceptanceCriteria: All required images exist under "frontend/public/assets/generated" and are referenced via "/assets/generated/..." paths.
Store items display a thumbnail image for each neon light color.
XP coin pickups use coin images (or in-world sprites) that visually differentiate 1/5/10/20 values.

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in "backend/main.mo" (no additional backend services).

Do not edit immutable frontend paths: "frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts", "frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.tsx", "frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts", "frontend/src/main.tsx", and any files under "frontend/src/components/ui"; compose them instead.
Use English for any user-facing text.
Store navigation must be implemented from the existing Main Menu page ("frontend/src/pages/MainMenu.tsx") via a button that routes to the Store page.
Persist permanent unlocks per Internet Identity principal.
nonGoals: Implementing real-money payments for the neon lights.
Adding third-party authentication beyond Internet Identity.
Adding external databases or off-chain storage for XP/inventory.
Multiplayer syncing of coin positions/collection across players in real time beyond what the current game supports.

required: Neon light icon thumbnail set: red, blue, yellow, purple, orange, white, multicolor (neon-light-red.dim_256x256.png; neon-light-blue.dim_256x256.png; neon-light-yellow.dim_256x256.png; neon-light-purple.dim_256x256.png; neon-light-orange.dim_256x256.png; neon-light-white.dim_256x256.png; neon-light-multicolor.dim_256x256.png)
XP coin sprites for values 1, 5, 10, 20 with clear numeric marking (xp-coin-1.dim_128x128.png; xp-coin-5.dim_128x128.png; xp-coin-10.dim_128x128.png; xp-coin-20.dim_128x128.png)
